mission.title: prometheus: Migrate Prometheus APIs to OpenAPI/Swagger
mission.description: Migrate Prometheus APIs to OpenAPI/Swagger. Requested by 10+ users.

steps.title: Check current prometheus deployment
steps.description:
Verify your prometheus version and configuration:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n prometheus -l app.kubernetes.io/name=prometheus
helm list -n prometheus 2>/dev/null || echo "Not installed via Helm"
```
This feature requires a working prometheus installation.

steps.title: Review prometheus configuration
steps.description:
Inspect the relevant prometheus configuration:
```bash
kubectl get all -n prometheus -l app.kubernetes.io/name=prometheus
kubectl get configmap -n prometheus -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=prometheus
```
IMO prometheus should have live/interactive swagger doc for the API ala: http://petstore.swagger.io

steps.title: Apply the fix for Migrate Prometheus APIs to OpenAPI/Swagger

unfortunately that is a lot of details and I do not think this is how we should proceed. There are a *lot* of subtle things in openapi and I do not believe that crafting openapi "out of the blue" can give a fully correct result.

The objective here is to have openapi
```yaml
How it looks like for OpenAPI-generated schema:
```

steps.title: Verify the feature works
steps.description:
Test that the new capability is working as expected:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n prometheus -l app.kubernetes.io/name=prometheus
kubectl get events -n prometheus --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm the feature described in "Migrate Prometheus APIs to OpenAPI/Swagger" is functioning correctly.

resolution.summary:
Hello, thanks for your work.

unfortunately that is a lot of details and I do not think this is how we should proceed. There are a *lot* of subtle things in openapi and I do not believe that crafting openapi "out of the blue" can give a fully correct result.

The objective here is to have openapi generating go code somehow, and then use that generated code to implement the actual API.
codeSnippets: How it looks like for OpenAPI-generated schema:

prerequisites.description: A running Kubernetes cluster with prometheus installed or the issue environment reproducible.
